jobs.title: Senior Principal Product Manager, Coach Journey
jobs.jd_full:
Twin Health

At Twin Health, we empower people to improve and prevent chronic metabolic diseases, like type 2 diabetes and obesity, with a new standard of care. Twin Health is the only company applying AI Digital Twin technology exclusively toward metabolic health.

We start by building a dynamic model of each person’s metabolism — drawing on thousands of data points from CGMs, smartwatches, and meal logs — that maps their personal path to better health. Guided by a dedicated clinical care team, our members have lowered their A1C below the diabetes range, achieved lasting weight loss, and reduced or even eliminated medications, all while living healthier, happier lives.

Working here

Our team at Twin Health is passionate, talented, and united by a shared purpose: to improve the metabolic health and happiness of our members. We believe in empowering every Twin to make a meaningful impact for our members, our clients, and each other, while enjoying a supportive, collaborative work environment.

Twin has been recognized not only for our innovation but also for our culture, including: Innovator of the Year by the Employer Health Innovation Roundtable (EHIR), selected to CB Insights’ Digital Health 150, and named one of Newsweek’s Top Most Loved Workplace® .

With more than $100 million raised in recent funding, including a $53 million Series E round in 2025 led by Maj Invest, and a $50 million investment in 2023 led by Temasek, Twin is scaling rapidly across the U.S. and globally. Backed by leading venture firms like ICONIQ Growth, Sequoia, Sofina, Temasek, and Peak XV, we are building the most impactful digital health company in the world.

Join us as we reinvent the standard of care in metabolic health.

Opportunity

We are looking for a Senior Principal Product Manager to lead Coach Copilot, the AI powered workspace our health coaches use every day to manage their member panel and deliver personalized, high quality care at scale. Coach Copilot summarizes each member’s behavior and adherence to the Twin program, powers end to end telehealth visit management, and surfaces the right members at the right time for coach intervention.

In this role, you will own the strategy, roadmap, and execution of the Coach Copilot experience, with a relentless focus on two outcomes: improving the quality of every coaching interaction to drive better health for our members, and dramatically increasing the efficiency of our care team. This is an ideal opportunity for a strategic, user centered product leader who is passionate about transforming healthcare through applied AI.

You’ll work closely with design, engineering, data science, AI/ML, coaches, and clinical leadership to identify high impact opportunities, prioritize solutions that address real world clinical needs, and drive alignment across cross functional teams.

Responsibilities

  • Own the strategy, roadmap, and execution for Coach Copilot, enabling health coaches to deliver high quality, personalized care at scale.
  • Deliver AI powered workflows, interventions, smart drafts, and summaries that improve coaching quality, increase program adherence, and drive better health outcomes while eliminating manual, low value work.
  • Develop a deep understanding of clinical workflows by partnering closely with coaches, clinical leaders, and cross functional stakeholders to identify high impact opportunities.
  • Translate complex clinical and operational problems into intuitive product experiences and detailed product requirements, challenging assumptions and influencing stakeholders when needed.
  • Balance immediate operational fires with long-term architectural bets that allow Twin to scale to hundreds of thousands of members without adding linear headcount.
  • Define and track weekly success metrics, measure product impact, and continuously iterate based on user feedback and clinical outcomes.
  • Partner with Design, Engineering, Data Science, and AI/ML teams to create seamless experiences that fit naturally into clinicians’ daily workflows.
  • Drive alignment across executive leadership and cross functional teams to ensure Coach Copilot advances the company’s AI, clinical, and business strategy.
  • Other duties as assigned.

Qualifications

  • Experience: 8+ years of product management experience. Experience in healthcare technology, clinical platforms, or AI powered products is a plus but not required.
  • Strategic Product Experience: you have a proven track record of owning a product area from “Vision to Value” with measurable business impact and experience building complex AI powered experiences, ideally leveraging LLMs. You proactively identify opportunities and shape product direction rather than simply executing stakeholder requests.
  • Operational Product Thinker: You excel at untangling complex problems, defining clear product strategy, and translating ambiguity into actionable product requirements. You challenge assumptions and push back when needed to ensure the team is solving the highest impact problems.
  • Data-Driven Mindset: You don’t just measure output; you use data to prioritize investments; you define success metrics and continuously iterate until you hit them.
  • Execution Excellence: You demonstrated your ability to lead agile teams and manage the product development lifecycle end to end.

Compensation and Benefits

The compensation range for this position is $205,000 - $250,000 annually.

Twin has an ambitious vision to empower people to live healthier and happier lives, and to achieve this purpose, we need the very best people to enhance our cutting-edge technology and medical science, deliver the best possible care, and turn our passion into value for our members, partners and investors. We are committed to delivering an outstanding culture and experience for every Twin employee through a company based on the values of passion, talent, and trust. We offer comprehensive benefits and perks in line with these principles, as well as a high level of flexibility for every Twin

  • A competitive compensation package in line with leading technology companies
  • A remote and accomplished global team
  • Opportunity for equity participation
  • Unlimited vacation with manager approval
  • 16 weeks of 100% paid parental leave for delivering parents; 8 weeks of 100% paid parental leave for non-delivering parents
  • 100% Employer sponsored healthcare, dental, and vision for you, and 80% coverage for your family; Health Savings Account and Flexible Spending Account options
  • 401k retirement savings plan

Salary range for US jobs

US Salary Range

$205,000—$250,000 USD

We have been made aware of fraudulent interview requests being sent using the Twin Health’s name. All communications will come from official Twin Health channels and a twinhealth.com email address. We will never ask you to complete a text interview or request financial details during the interview process.

jobs.title: Technical Project Manager (Remote)
jobs.jd_full:
RainFocus, one of the most innovative software companies, is in search of an exceptional Technical Project Manager.

About RainFocus

RainFocus cares about its employees, customers, and the world in which we live. Our rapidly growing team serves Fortune 500 companies like Adobe, Cisco, IBM, Oracle, VMware, and others to prepare and execute in-person, virtual, and hybrid events, across the world. Those events are delivered through our industry-disrupting software platform, with groundbreaking business intelligence, to elevate the attendee experience, streamline event operations, and accelerate marketing results. We are well-funded, growing fast, and building a company that is changing the market — it will be challenging, fun and exciting.

About the Role

This role is part of our Business Systems team, working closely with stakeholders across the business to build, refine, and clean up tickets for our Salesforce-centric tech stack, while also helping us extend AI-driven improvements across the broader organization. You’ll blend project management with product-style thinking. Quickly learn how a wide range of business applications work, translate stakeholder needs into well-scoped technical tickets for developers, and help us drive real, measurable change through the use of AI.

Our team is fast-moving and expanding beyond Salesforce into our broader tech stack, providing an excellent opportunity to shape how requests are captured, prioritized, and delivered, and to help the business adopt AI in practical, high-impact ways. Key applications include but are not limited to: Salesforce, Slack, Gong, Claude, Gemini, N8N, Clay, Certinia, Craft, HighSpot, NetSuite, Lucid, and more.

Essential Responsibilities:

  • Ticket Building & Backlog Management: Build, refine, and clean up tickets for the Business Systems Admins and Developers, writing clear, well-scoped technical requirements that developers can act on without unnecessary back-and-forth.

  • AI-Driven Process Improvement: Identify opportunities to apply AI tools and workflows to solve business problems, and help drive adoption of AI-based solutions across teams. Hands-on AI experience is a must for this role.

  • Cross-Functional Stakeholder Management: Interface with stakeholders across multiple business units to intake, vet, and prioritize requests, translating business needs into clear technical tasks for the development team. Act as the go-between for developers and business systems admins, reaching out to the business to gather clearer requirements or feedback on a ticket so the technical team can stay focused.

  • Product & Project Ownership: Own the roadmap and backlog for the Business Systems team’s initiatives, balancing day-to-day project delivery with longer-term product thinking about what to build next and why.

  • Cross-Platform Fluency: Quickly learn how a wide range of software applications work (Salesforce and beyond) well enough to scope requests, spot integration or AI opportunities, and ask the right technical questions.

  • Agile Process Participation: Participate in sprint ceremonies (standups, grooming, retrospectives) to keep work visible and moving, supporting the team’s agile process.

Required Skills & Experience:

  • Technical Project/Product Management: Experience managing technical projects, owning products, or backlogs (ideally for a systems, IT, or business applications team).

  • High Tech Fluency: While coding is not required, you must be able to quickly understand how different software platforms work and connect (e.g., via APIs), and be comfortable learning new systems on the fly.

  • Hands-On AI Experience: Demonstrated experience using AI tools (e.g., Claude, Gemini, Slackbot or similar) to solve real business problems.

  • Technical Communication: Strong ability to translate business needs into clear, well-organized technical tickets and requirements that developers can execute against.

  • Stakeholder Management: Proven ability to work across multiple business units, filter competing priorities, and communicate clearly with both technical and non-technical audiences.

Desired Skills:

  • Familiarity or direct experience with the Salesforce ecosystem and how it integrates with third-party tools.

  • Experience with Slackbot, Claude, Clay, Rovo and/or Gemini, including enterprise administration, workflow automation, or building Claude skills or agents for business use.

  • Experience writing technical tickets or requirements for software developers.

  • Familiarity with agile delivery frameworks (Scrum/Kanban) and tools like Jira or similar.

Personal Characteristics & Success Measures

Personal Characteristics

  • Proactive Problem-Solver: An innovative thinker who doesn’t wait for direction but independently seeks out ways to optimize workflows and eliminate team bottlenecks.

  • Self-Motivated Learner: A self-starter who stays up to date on the rapidly changing landscape of AI capabilities, LLMs, and internal tool integrations.

  • Great Attention to Detail: Meticulous with project tracking, dependency mapping, and ensuring enterprise data compliance.

  • Collaborative Partner: Highly enthusiastic, high-energy, and ready to act as a true strategic member of our Business Systems team.

Success Measures

  • Clear, Actionable Tickets: Business Systems tickets are consistently well-scoped, clearly written, and ready for developers to act on with minimal clarification needed.

  • AI-Driven Impact: Deliver concrete examples of AI tools or workflows deployed to solve business problems and drive measurable improvements.

  • Bottleneck Reduction: Proactively identify and deploy automated or AI-driven solutions that reduce friction for stakeholders across the business.

  • Seamless Collaboration: Act as a reliable partner to both stakeholders and developers, keeping requests, tickets, and system updates moving forward smoothly.

Location

This remote role can be located anywhere in the United States of America.

Why work at RainFocus?

At RainFocus we delight millions of attendees at large-scale events by delivering better insights, experiences, and marketing. We were able to pivot our product and services offering in 2020 to continue growing and serving new clients and events.

As a member of the RainFocus team, you will have the opportunity to experience first-hand the impact of our platform at events around the world. Additionally, RainFocus offers competitive salaries, competitive benefits, 401k, generous PTO, and countless other team building activities.

What are you waiting for? Apply today! We need more talented, hard-working, fun-loving team members just like yourself!

We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses and identifying potential inconsistencies or verification signals in application materials based on available information. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

jobs.title: Project Manager, Healthcare and Pharmaceutical Advertising
jobs.jd_full:
When you join Precision AQ, you become part of a team united by a shared purpose: helping healthcare innovations reach the patients who need them most. Our work supports the launch of new treatments, advances scientific communication, and helps clients navigate complex healthcare challenges. We believe our best work comes from collaboration, diverse perspectives, and a culture where great ideas can come from anyone.

At Precision AQ, success is built on teamwork. We value people who are curious, accountable, and committed to supporting those around them. Our culture is grounded in collaboration, mutual respect, and a shared drive to deliver exceptional results for our clients. Here, you’ll work alongside talented colleagues who challenge and inspire one another while making a meaningful impact across healthcare.

The Project Management team is at the center of agency operations, connecting people, processes, and priorities to keep work moving forward. We are strategic problem solvers and trusted partners who collaborate across functions to deliver high-quality work on time and within budget.

As a Project Manager, you will lead projects from planning through execution, partnering closely with account, creative, editorial, and production teams to ensure successful delivery. You’ll play a critical role in balancing timelines, resources, budgets, and stakeholder expectations while helping teams navigate challenges and maintain momentum. This is an opportunity to work in a highly collaborative environment, contribute to a wide range of healthcare and pharmaceutical initiatives, and make a measurable impact on client success.

About You

  • You are a proactive problem solver who enjoys bringing structure to complex projects and competing priorities.

  • You are a strong communicator who can collaborate effectively with individuals across multiple functions and levels.

  • You thrive in fast-paced environments and can adapt quickly when priorities shift.

  • You are highly organized and detail-oriented, with a commitment to delivering quality work on time and within budget.

  • You enjoy leading meetings, driving accountability, and keeping teams aligned around shared goals.

  • You take a solutions-oriented approach and can identify risks, evaluate options, and keep projects moving forward.

About the Role

Reporting to senior project management leadership, you will support the planning, execution, and delivery of client programs and projects. You will develop project plans, timelines, and workflows while partnering closely with account teams and other stakeholders to ensure alignment on objectives, budgets, and deliverables. You will facilitate internal meetings, manage project status communications, and oversee workflow processes from kickoff through final delivery. Additionally, you will monitor project progress, identify potential risks, and help keep projects on track.

Required Experience

  • Bachelor’s degree required.

  • Minimum of 2 years of project management experience, including project planning, execution, and delivery.

  • Experience managing multiple projects, timelines, and stakeholders simultaneously.

  • Proficiency with Microsoft Office and project management software or workflow management tools.

  • Strong meeting facilitation, communication, and organizational skills.

  • Ability to identify risks, manage priorities, and drive projects to completion.

Helpful Experience

  • Advertising agency experience.

  • Experience supporting pharmaceutical or healthcare clients.

  • Familiarity with digital, print, and broadcast production processes.

  • Experience working within cross-functional client service environments.

Precision is required by law in some states or cities to include a reasonable estimate of the compensation range for this role. This compensation range takes into account the wide range of factors that are considered in making compensation decisions including but not limited to: skill sets, experience and training, licensure and certifications, and other business and organizational needs. The disclosed range estimate has not been adjusted for the applicable geographic differential associated with the location at which the position may be filled. At Precision, it is not typical for an individual to be hired at or near the top of the range for their role and compensation decisions are dependent on the facts and circumstances of each case.  This role is also eligible for a discretionary annual bonus, health insurance, retirement savings benefits, life insurance and disability benefits, parental leave, and paid time off for sick leave and vacation, among other benefits.

Reasonable estimate of the current range

$60,000—$90,000 USD

Any data provided as a part of this application will be stored in accordance with our Privacy Policy. For CA applicants, please also refer to our CA Privacy Notice.

Precision Medicine Group is an Equal Opportunity Employer. Employment decisions are made without regard to race, color, age, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability, veteran status or other characteristics protected by law.

If you are an individual with a disability and require a reasonable accommodation to complete any part of the application process or are limited in the ability or unable to access or use this online application process and need an alternative method for applying, you may contact Precision Medicine Group at myHR@precisionmedicinegrp.com.

Recruitment Fraud Warning: Please be aware that fraudulent individuals and websites may impersonate Precision Medicine Group or its affiliates and attempt to obtain personal, financial, or banking information through fake job postings or employment offers. Precision Medicine Group will never request payment, banking details, or other sensitive financial information as part of the recruitment process. If you believe you have been targeted by a recruitment scam, we encourage you to report the incident to your local law enforcement authorities, consumer protection agency, or relevant cybercrime reporting organization in your country. Please also notify us at myHR@precisionmedicinegrp.com so we can investigate and take appropriate action.

jobs.title: Website Project Manager (Remote | South Africa) - 3 month contract
jobs.jd_full:
Welcome to Huble 👋

Huble is an international digital business & CRM consultancy and an Elite HubSpot Partner. We help businesses scale and optimize their sales, marketing, and service operations through cutting-edge digital strategies and CRM-driven transformation. Our clients range from mid-sized businesses to global enterprises, and we pride ourselves on our ability to deliver strategic and creative consulting and advanced technology solutions.

We are looking for a Website Project Manager to lead the end-to-end planning, execution, and delivery of website projects built on the HubSpot CMS. You will manage project scope, timelines, and budgets, coordinate cross-functional teams, and ensure an exceptional experience for all stakeholders.

🚀 Here are a few of the tasks you’ll get to work on:

  • Scheduling & Budgeting: Build comprehensive project plans, allocate resources with Resource Managers, and manage budgets to prevent under- or over-servicing.
  • Project Administration & Tracking: Maintain documentation (Risk Logs, Change Logs, Progress Reports) and proactively monitor deliverables to keep projects on scope and contract.
  • Stakeholder & Risk Management: Lead client and internal meetings, manage deadlines, resolve technical/operational roadblocks, and mitigate project risks.
  • Quality & Communication: Maintain clear progress reporting, ensure timely communications, and drive continuous improvement.

🏗️ To excel in this role, you should have the following:

  • Bachelor’s degree or equivalent experience in a related field.
  • Senior-level project management experience in an agency, consultancy, or software development environment handling digital marketing and web projects.
  • Formal training or proficiency in Project Management Methodologies.
  • Experience with project management platforms (e.g., Monday.com, Teamleader, TeamGantt) and Google Workspace.
  • Experience with HubSpot/CRMs, IT, or complex web builds.

⚡️ These personal qualities are key to your success:

  • Strong analytical, organisational, and problem-solving skills with high attention to detail.
  • Excellent written and verbal communication skills with strong interpersonal presence.
  • Ability to thrive under pressure in fast-paced, dynamic environments.

🛣️ Interview Process:

  • Video Interview/Intro Call (15 min)
  • 1st Interview (30 min)
  • Assessment
  • Presentation Interview (60 min)
  • Final Interview (30 min)

🎁 As part of the remuneration package, you will receive the following

  • 🌍 Remote work - Enjoy the freedom of remote work within the country of employment.
  • 🔌 Loadshedding support - We provide a power station to help you during load shedding.
  • 🪑 Home office budget - Create your optimal workspace with a budget for a desk and chair.
  • 💻 IT Equipment - Choose between a MacBook or Windows laptop, plus a monitor and mouse.
  • 🌴 Generous annual leave - Take advantage of 18 days of annual leave, plus 2 extra days for special occasions like your birthday.
  • 🧘 Flexible schedule - We understand and accommodate your personal needs, whether it’s for leisure or family commitments.
  • 👕 Huble Swag - Show off your Huble pride with our awesome swag.
  • 🏥 Medical Aid - Get subsidized cover on your health related needs.

🤝 Keen to Huble with us?

If you are ready to take on this exciting opportunity and join our team, we would love to hear from you. Apply today and let’s Huble together!

NB - Please note that candidates must have the legal right to work and current residency in South Africa.

Please note that we have not enlisted the help of external recruiters for this role. If contacted by someone outside of Huble who does not have the @hubledigital.com email domain, please inform us immediately. Thank you!

jobs.title: Senior Oracle ERP Service Delivery Manager
jobs.jd_full:
Company Description

For over 30 years, Version 1 has been a leading Oracle partner specialising in Enterprise Applications, delivering ERP, HCM and EPM solutions that enable organisations to transform and modernise their core systems.

Combining deep domain expertise with end-to-end delivery and managed services, we support some of the UK and Ireland’s largest organisations in driving business value through Oracle technology.

  • Top-tier Oracle partner across Database, Platform, ERP, and Managed Services
  • 37 UKOUG awards over the last 3 years
  • 13 awards - UK Oracle User Group (UKOUG) Community Awards 2026 (4 Gold, 5 Silver, 4 Bronze)
  • Certified Great Place to Work
  • Recognised as a leader in AI & digital transformation consulting with global delivery footprint

With 3,800+ people and revenues exceeding €347m/£302m, Version 1 is a market leader in Oracle, Microsoft, and AWS consulting services – and one of the fastest-growing digital consultancies in Europe.

Job Description

Senior Oracle ERP Service Delivery Manager

Would you like the opportunity to further develop your expertise across Oracle Cloud ERP, HCM, EPM and Oracle SaaS Managed Services?

How would you like to join an award-winning Oracle Partner with over 700 dedicated Oracle consultants across the UK, Ireland, US, Australia, India, and Slovenia, delivering transformational Finance, HR, and Supply Chain solutions to some of the world’s leading organisations?

At Version 1, we are seeking an experienced Oracle SaaS Service Delivery Manager to join our growing Enterprise Applications Practice.

As a trusted advisor to our customers, you will be responsible for ensuring the successful delivery and ongoing management of Oracle Cloud services. You will act as the customer’s advocate within Version 1, driving service excellence, operational performance, customer satisfaction, and business growth.

This is a highly rewarding role offering the opportunity to work with cutting-edge Oracle Cloud technologies, engage with senior stakeholders, and support customers across multiple industries on their digital transformation journeys.

Key Responsibilities

As an Oracle SaaS Service Delivery Manager, you will:

  • Report directly to the Head of Service Delivery.
  • Own the end-to-end delivery of Oracle Cloud Managed Services across ERP, HCM, EPM and related SaaS applications.
  • Act as the primary point of contact for customers, ensuring service commitments and business objectives are consistently achieved.
  • Build and maintain strong relationships with customer stakeholders, becoming a trusted partner for Oracle Cloud service delivery and optimisation.
  • Lead regular Service Review Meetings, presenting service performance, SLA achievements, risk management activities, and improvement initiatives.
  • Manage customer escalations, major incidents, service risks, and business-critical issues through to successful resolution.
  • Drive Continual Service Improvement (CSI) initiatives, identifying opportunities to enhance service quality, efficiency, and customer value.
  • Provide governance and oversight for Oracle SaaS support teams, technical leads, functional consultants, and service delivery resources.
  • Ensure effective capacity planning, resource management, and forecasting of service costs, revenues, and operational demand.
  • Collaborate with Oracle practice leadership to define and execute service delivery strategies aligned to customer and business goals.
  • Monitor and report on key service metrics, customer satisfaction scores, utilisation, profitability, and operational performance.
  • Identify opportunities for service expansion, optimisation projects, cloud enhancements, and additional consulting engagements within existing accounts.
  • Ensure compliance with ITIL best practices, ISO 20000 standards, and Version 1 service governance frameworks.
  • Support service transition activities for new Oracle Cloud implementations moving into managed services.
  • Promote knowledge sharing, operational excellence, and business continuity across service delivery teams.
  • Balance and optimise the three pillars of service success:
    • Customer Satisfaction
    • Employee Engagement
    • Commercial Performance & Profitability

What Success Looks Like

  • Consistently high customer satisfaction and retention rates.
  • Successful delivery of Oracle Cloud Managed Services within agreed SLAs and KPIs.
  • Proactive identification and execution of service improvement opportunities.
  • Strong commercial performance through effective customer growth and service expansion.
  • High performing, engaged, and collaborative delivery teams.
  • Trusted advisor relationships with customer stakeholders and business leaders.

This role is ideal for a Service Delivery Manager with experience managing Oracle Cloud ERP, HCM, EPM or SaaS Managed Services environments, who is passionate about delivering exceptional customer outcomes while driving continuous improvement and business growth.

Qualifications

  • Proven experience delivering customer-first outcomes within ERP transformation and managed services environments.
  • Strong leadership skills with the ability to inspire, motivate, and develop high-performing teams.
  • Commercially astute with experience identifying and driving revenue growth opportunities.
  • Experience supporting bids, proposals, and service delivery engagements.
  • Strong analytical and problem-solving capabilities.
  • Confident challenging assumptions and asking the right questions to drive better outcomes.
  • Curious mindset with a commitment to continuous learning and improvement.
  • Experience working within structured, process-driven delivery environments.
  • Resource management experience including performance management, workload allocation, team development, and knowledge management.
  • Expertise in financial forecasting, including team costs, gross profit, recurring revenue, and project revenue forecasting.
  • Strong understanding of software configuration and release management practices.
  • Excellent verbal, written, and stakeholder communication skills.
  • Self-starter with a proactive approach to problem-solving, collaboration, and escalation management when required.

Additional Information

Why Version 1?

You will:

  • Join a global Oracle practice of 700+ specialists, supported by 500+ certified professionals across the Oracle ecosystem.
  • Work on large-scale, multi-pillar Oracle Cloud transformation programmes for some of the UK’s most recognised organisations.
  • Collaborate with a high-performing, market-leading Oracle team renowned for delivering complex enterprise transformation projects.
  • Be part of an organisation that provides end-to-end Oracle services, including consulting and advisory, implementation, integration, cloud infrastructure, and managed services.
  • Leverage our deep Oracle Cloud Infrastructure (OCI) expertise to deliver scalable, secure, and high-performing cloud solutions for enterprise clients.
  • Accelerate your career through our Strength in Balance and leadership development programmes, supported by quarterly Pathways career reviews.
  • Benefit from a substantial training and certification budget, helping you stay at the forefront of Oracle technologies and industry best practices.
  • Enjoy meaningful recognition through our Annual Excellence Awards, Call-Out recognition platform, and quarterly profit-share scheme.
  • Influence testing strategy, delivery quality, and business outcomes across some of the UK’s most significant ERP transformation programmes.
  • Work with prestigious clients, including organisations such as the Bank of England and Birmingham City Council.
  • Take advantage of our flexible hybrid working model, combining remote delivery with occasional client or hub-based collaboration.
  • Access enhanced wellbeing and family support through our maternity, paternity, and Moments that Matter policies.
  • Be part of a purpose-driven culture with opportunities to contribute to ESG, CSR, DEI, volunteering, and community initiatives that create a positive social impact.

At Version 1, you’ll combine industry-leading Oracle expertise, meaningful career progression, and the opportunity to deliver transformational outcomes for some of the world’s most recognised organisations.

Benefits include:

  • Quarterly performance-related profit share
  • Hybrid / Remote working flexibility
  • Pension, private healthcare, life assurance cover
  • Certified Great Place to Work for 10+ years running.

This is hybrid based with occasional travel to your nearest base office.

Krish Bharadwaj, Global Talent Acquisition – Enterprise Applications (Oracle Cloud - ERP, HCM, EPM)
